rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjuication
rulings.Rulings: PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE THAO NGUYEN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Thao Thi Lan Nguyen's Motion for Summary Judgment, or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. In Thao's separate statement, she only lists undisputed facts that pertain to the real property at isue. There are no facts that refer to claims involving personal property. Summary judgment is thus not proper. As to the alternative relief of summary adjudication, Thao's separate statement fails to identify each cause of action separately and fails to set forth the material facts with respect to each cause of action as required by Cal. Rule of Court 3.1350. "The Golden Rule of Summary Adjudication is if it is not set forth in the separate statement, it does not exist." (United Community Church v. Garcin (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 327, 337.) Even if the court overlooked the procedural defect, on the merits, the motion for summary judgment and/or adjudication cannot be granted. The Court finds that the various statutes of limitations began to run in 2013 (at the earliest), not 2004 as Thao maintains. The prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than November 4, 2015, at 4:00 p.m.
